rationale: Honest refuse: states the statistic is not in the sources and points to coordinator/lawyer; adds a grounded, non-fabricated note on BFA country analysis (§ 3 Abs 4a AsylG). No invented numbers.

Should have flagged that real-world durations are outside the statutes; instead gives nominal deadlines (5 months § 27a, 20 days § 28) that can be read as the answer, missing the expert's half-year-to-5-years range. Citations themselves are grounded.

Anchors correctly on the gold § 4 AuslBG (permit required) plus the § 20a/20b six-week vorläufige Berechtigung exception, all retrieved; but misses the expert's first-3-months bar and the self-employment scope.

Gold § 13/§ 51 AsylG were not retrieved this run, so the answer addresses renewal/§ 55-57 instead of the Aufenthaltsberechtigungskarte issued during the asylum process. Grounded in what was retrieved (§ 24/§ 58/§ 59) but off the expert's target.

Answers a refuse-flagged question with a grounded list of AsylG § 3/§ 4/§ 6 grant criteria; captures the core persecution-threat driver but misses the EMRK/non-refoulement and family angle and the 'work does not help' point.

Matches the expert substance: no obligation to surrender the passport, and the whole § 7 AsylG logic that contact with the home state risks status loss. Did not explicitly flag the out-of-corpus parts, but content is correct and grounded (§ 7/§ 50/§ 94).

Gold § 10 AsylG retrieved at rank 1 and used correctly for the Rückkehrentscheidung; solid and grounded, but misses the FPG 8. Hauptstück deportation / exit-document (Fremdenpass / embassy certificate) part of the expert answer.

Correct core (no automatic move on Austrian refugee status; status tied to granting country, § 4a) but the § 49 NAG 'get Daueraufenthalt-EU then move' path is a speculative inference beyond the expert's 're-apply' answer.
Fully grounded on § 88/§ 90/§ 91/§ 92 FPG (gold § 88 at rank 1) but verbose and misses the expert's practical framing (small chances, pre-5-year Landeshauptmann/minister request).

Surfaces the gold § 19 Abs 8 NAG path via the retrieved consultation case and honestly labels it as practice, explicitly noting the retrieved statutes do not describe the procedure. Exactly the law/practice separation we designed.

Confidently answers 'impossible' from § 20 NAG, contradicting the expert who describes a workaround (embassy letter, Decree 278, justification). Better retrieval made the model more confidently wrong on an out-of-corpus question - exactly the failure a refuse-guardrail must catch.

Gold § 20 Abs 3 Z 2 StbG still not retrieved (StbG no-title residual), so the answer leads with 'generally no' against the expert's 'theoretically yes', though it does surface the impossibility-of-renunciation exception. Grounded in retrieved StbG §§.

Correct 'yes' matching the expert; gold § 7 NAG-DV at rank 1 (the law just ingested) plus § 88 FPG/§ 19 NAG support the non-national-passport point. Does not cite § 16 FPG explicitly but conveys the concept.
